description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a tiny deployment bundle that will later be copied to several IoT devices.  
Inside the terminal, do the following steps (roughly 3–4 commands are sufficient):

1. Create a directory called iot_deployment directly under your home directory: /home/user/iot_deployment  
   • Make sure the directory itself ends up with octal permissions 750 (rwx r-x ---).

2. Inside that directory create two files:
   a) deploy.sh — a bash script that prints the single line  
      Deploying to edge node...  
      (no extra whitespace or newline characters other than the trailing newline automatically added when you save the file).  
   b) config.json — a JSON file containing exactly:  
      {"mode":"edge","version":"1.0"}  
      followed by a trailing newline.

3. Adjust permissions so that after you finish:  
   • /home/user/iot_deployment/deploy.sh has octal mode 750  
   • /home/user/iot_deployment/config.json has octal mode 640  

4. Verification log (very important):  
   After setting the permissions, generate a log file called /home/user/iot_deployment/permission_audit.log.  
   The file must contain exactly three lines, each in the format  

   <name> <octal_permissions>  

   where  
   • <name> is one of: deploy.sh, config.json, dir  
   • <octal_permissions> is the three-digit permission you set (e.g., 750).

   The required final content of permission_audit.log is therefore, in any order:  
   deploy.sh 750  
   config.json 640  
   dir 750  

   No extra spaces, blank lines, or other text.

You do NOT have root access, so use only commands available to an unprivileged user.  
When everything is correct, the automated tests will:

• Check that /home/user/iot_deployment exists and is a directory.  
• Verify the exact file contents of deploy.sh and config.json.  
• Confirm that the directory and files have the required octal permissions.  
• Read /home/user/iot_deployment/permission_audit.log and ensure it has exactly the three required lines and nothing else.

Hint: “dir” in permission_audit.log refers to the iot_deployment directory itself.  
Achieve the above using as few commands as you like, but no more than four are necessary.
truth:
Expected artifacts and their final state:

1. Directory /home/user/iot_deployment  
   • Permissions: 750 (rwx r-x ---)  
   • Owner & group: user:user (default; the agent cannot change this)

2. File /home/user/iot_deployment/deploy.sh  
   • Content (including trailing newline):  
     Deploying to edge node...\n  
   • Permissions: 750 (rwx r-x ---)

3. File /home/user/iot_deployment/config.json  
   • Content (including trailing newline):  
     {"mode":"edge","version":"1.0"}\n  
   • Permissions: 640 (rw- r-- ---)

4. File /home/user/iot_deployment/permission_audit.log  
   • Content (order of lines may vary; exactly these three newline-terminated lines, no extra lines/spaces):  
     deploy.sh 750\n  
     config.json 640\n  
     dir 750\n  
   • Permissions do not matter to the grader; default (644) is fine.
name: task_000000_e15329a0
